why: why isn't my created element showing on screen
how: how to create html elements dynamically
how to append child to div
output: failed to execute appendChild on Node

1_beginner_explanation.summary: You can use JavaScript to build entirely new HTML tags from scratch and inject them into the page. The process is always 3 steps: 1) Create an empty element in memory (`document.createElement`). 2) Add text, classes, or attributes to it. 3) Attach it to the visible DOM tree (`parent.appendChild` or `parent.append`).
1_beginner_explanation.analogy: Creating an element is like building a piece of IKEA furniture in your garage. `document.createElement('div')` builds it. Adding `.innerText` is painting it. But until you physically carry it from the garage and place it in your living room (`appendChild`), nobody will see it.

2_internal_mechanism.execution_flow: Calling `document.createElement` instantiates a detached C++ DOM Node in the Heap. It takes up memory but has no parent and is not part of the Render Tree. When you call `appendChild`, the engine links the pointers, recalculates the CSS layout (Reflow), and paints the new pixels on screen.
2_internal_mechanism.memory: Constantly creating and appending elements one-by-one in a loop causes terrible performance due to repeated Reflows. Best practice is to append all new elements to a `DocumentFragment` in memory, and append the fragment to the DOM once.

3_real_world_example.use_case: Adding a new item to a to-do list when the user submits a form.

4_common_confusion.trap:
Using `innerHTML += '
'` inside a loop. While this works, it destroys the existing DOM nodes inside that container and recreates them entirely from scratch on every loop iteration. This wipes out event listeners and destroys performance.
4_common_confusion.fix: Always use `createElement` + `appendChild`, or `insertAdjacentHTML` if you must use strings.

6_edge_case.quirk: If you `appendChild` an element that is ALREADY on the screen, JavaScript does not create a copy. It moves the element from its old location to the new location.
6_edge_case.code:
const box = document.querySelector('#box');
document.body.appendChild(box); // Literally teleports the box to the bottom of the body
